programs.name: Med-QUEST Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) Waivers
programs.what_it_does: These waivers provide home and community-based services as an alternative to institutional care. Services include personal care, adult day care, and homemaker assistance to help seniors remain at home.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and disabled individuals eligible for nursing home level care but preferring home-based services.
programs.benefit_value: Tailored services up to nursing facility cost limits; annual waiver slots limited, e.g., thousands served statewide.

programs.name: Hawai‘i PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: Hawai‘i PACE offers comprehensive medical and social services to eligible frail elders. It includes adult day health care, transportation, home care, and hospitalization to enable community living.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified for nursing home level care, able to live safely in the community with supports.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitated care model; covers Medicare and Medicaid services without copays for dual eligibles.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for low-income beneficiaries. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and cost-sharing; SLMB and QI cover Part B premiums.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries; income limits vary (e.g., QMB up to 100% FPL, SLMB 120%, QI 135% plus asset tests).
programs.benefit_value: Premium payments ($174.70 Part B/month in 2024) plus cost-sharing relief; saves $2,000+ annually per enrollee.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly EBT benefits to buy healthy food. Administered by BESSD for low-income households including seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+, disabled, or aged/blind/disabled with income/assets below limits.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per eligible senior household; maximum benefits based on household size.

programs.name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP helps with home energy costs through cash grants or crisis aid. It prevents utility shutoffs and supports heating/cooling needs.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors; priority for those 60+, disabled, or with high energy burdens.
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants $200-1,000+ depending on need; crisis aid up to full bill payment.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP improves energy efficiency in low-income homes via insulation, sealing, and efficient appliances. It reduces energy costs and health risks from poor conditions.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors, disabled, and families; priority for elderly, children, and disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Free home upgrades worth $5,000-10,000; lifetime energy savings of $4,000+ per home.

programs.name: Sage PLUS (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Sage PLUS offers free Medicare counseling, education, and assistance. Counselors help with benefits, plans, appeals, and low-income aid.
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries, caregivers, and families seeking insurance guidance.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; helps save thousands via optimal plan selection.

programs.name: Home-Delivered Meals (via Area Agencies on Aging)
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutritious meals delivered to homebound seniors. Supports nutrition and independence through daily or weekly service.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income or at nutrition risk.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week at no or suggested $3-5 donation; serves 1,000+ daily statewide.

programs.name: Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care, training, and counseling for caregivers. Includes supplies and short-term relief to prevent burnout.
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of seniors 60+ or disabled adults 18-59.
programs.benefit_value: Respite up to $50/hour or cash allowances; training and support statewide.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid training jobs in community service. Helps unemployed low-income seniors gain skills and employment.
programs.benefit_value: 20 hours/week at minimum wage ($10.10/hour in HI); leads to unsubsidized jobs.

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal help for elder rights issues like abuse, benefits, housing. Includes advice, representation, and education.
programs.benefit_value: Free services worth $200+/hour; resolves benefits, guardianship cases.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program (LTCOP)
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities resolving complaints. Protects rights and improves quality of life.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving 80%+ complaints; statewide volunteer network.

programs.name: Kupuna Caregivers Program
programs.what_it_does: This state-funded program reimburses working caregivers up to $70 per day for services like adult day care, chore services, home-delivered meals, homemaker services, personal care, respite, or transportation to support their elderly family members. It aims to alleviate the financial burden on caregivers while allowing kupuna (elders) to remain at home.
programs.who_its_for: Working caregivers of Native Hawaiian elders (kupuna) aged 60 and older.
programs.whats_unique: Unique in the country as the first state initiative providing direct financial stipends to working caregivers for kupuna care, addressing Hawaii's rapidly aging population where seniors live longer than the national average.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $70 per day for eligible services.

programs.name: Kumu Kahi – Ke Ola Pono No Nā Kūpuna Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutritional and supportive services to enhance health, dignity, cultural identity, and lifelong learning for Native Hawaiian elders. Funded specifically through federal grants targeted at Native Hawaiians.
programs.who_its_for: Native Hawaiian elderly aged 60 and older.
programs.whats_unique: Exclusively for Native Hawaiians, preserving cultural identity in a state with a significant Native Hawaiian population; not available in other states.
programs.benefit_value: Nutritional and supportive services (specific monetary value not detailed; funded via Older Americans Act Title VI, Part B).

programs.name: Kumu Kahi – Native American Caregivers Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers support services to caregivers of Native Hawaiian elders, including respite and other assistance to promote elder well-being. Funded through targeted federal grants for Native American caregivers.
programs.who_its_for: Caregivers of Native Hawaiians aged 60 and older.
programs.whats_unique: Tailored to Native Hawaiian caregivers under the Native American framework, unique to Hawaii's indigenous population.
programs.benefit_value: Caregiver support services (specific monetary value not detailed; funded via Older Americans Act Title VI, Part C).

programs.name: Senior Citizen Rent Subsidy Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides financial assistance to low-income seniors to afford safe and decent housing through the Hawaii Public Housing Authority. Helps reduce housing costs significantly for eligible participants.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific rent subsidy administered by Hawaii Public Housing Authority, offering substantial discounts potentially saving hundreds annually; tailored to Hawaii's high housing costs.
programs.benefit_value: Financial assistance saving hundreds of dollars per year on rent.
